C-WORKFLOW-RESULT-PRESERVATION.Summary: Preserve tracked subagent result context and improve workflow protocol diagnostics for malformed top blocks and hard-stop outcomes.
C-WORKFLOW-RESULT-PRESERVATION.Problem: Workflow result validation can replace or obscure the original tracked subagent response with only a workflow validation or hard-stop error. This leaves the controller without the subagent handoff context, especially for BLOCKED and NEEDS_CONTEXT outcomes. The strict top-block parser also reports body text placed immediately after VVOC_STATUS, such as Findings text without a separating blank line, as a generic non-protocol line instead of explaining the missing blank line fix.

Goals.Goal: Preserve a bounded, controller-visible excerpt of the original tracked subagent result when protocol validation, state application, BLOCKED, or NEEDS_CONTEXT handling stops workflow automation.

Expose enough result context through workflow recovery surfaces, including work_item_list, so the controller can understand why a work item entered needs_context or blocked without relying on hidden child-session history.
Detect body text accidentally placed inside the strict protocol top block and report an actionable missing-blank-line diagnostic with a corrected format example.
Keep malformed protocol handling fail-closed while improving repair prompts so format-only repair can move body text below the required blank line without changing the subagent outcome.
Cover result excerpt preservation, hard stops, protocol diagnostics, repair guidance, and truncation behavior with deterministic tests.

NonGoals.NonGoal: Do not make the result parser silently accept malformed outputs that omit the blank line between protocol header and body.
Do not store unbounded raw subagent outputs in workflow state or persistence.
Do not change tracked agent names, allowed VVOC_STATUS values, work-item lifecycle states, or review-round transition semantics except where needed to attach bounded result context.
Do not change OpenCode task execution semantics outside the vvoc workflow plugin hooks.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: When a tracked subagent returns a valid BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT result with body text, the resulting hard-stop error includes a capped excerpt of the original result body or normalized result output with work item, agent, and status context.

When a work item enters blocked or needs_context, work_item_list exposes a bounded result excerpt sufficient for controller recovery, including implementer hard-stop context and reviewer NEEDS_CONTEXT context.
When result parsing fails after optional repair, RESULT_PROTOCOL_ERROR includes the protocol failure plus a capped excerpt of the original normalized subagent output.
When state application rejects an otherwise parsed result, RESULT_PROTOCOL_ERROR includes the state error plus a capped excerpt of the parsed result body or normalized output.

When a line such as Findings appears in the strict top block after VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID and VVOC_STATUS without a blank line, the protocol error identifies the missing blank line before the body and shows the corrected header-blank-line-body shape.
Result repair guidance for malformed top blocks explicitly instructs the subagent to move body text after a blank line while preserving the same work item, status, route, and underlying outcome.
Excerpt handling is deterministic, size-bounded, and marked as truncated when truncation occurs.
Existing valid strict result blocks, duplicate-field rejection, status validation, launch validation, review aggregation, and round-limit behavior continue to pass existing workflow tests.

VerificationIntent.ExpectedEvidence: Workflow tests include cases for hard-stop excerpt preservation, work_item_list recovery context, protocol-error excerpt preservation, missing-blank-line diagnostics for body text inside the strict top block, repair prompt guidance, and bounded truncation.

Assumptions.Assumption: OpenCode may surface a thrown tool.execute.after hook error instead of the original child task output; vvoc workflow errors therefore must carry enough context themselves.
Bounded excerpts are acceptable recovery context, but unbounded raw subagent output is not acceptable for persisted workflow state.
Existing secrets-redaction behavior remains responsible for provider-bound and tool-bound secret handling; this change only bounds and structures workflow result excerpts.
The strict protocol top block remains separated from freeform result body by a blank line.
